Gaseous.title: Gas giant
Gaseous.description: A planet composed mainly of gases with a molten rocky core. These planets are inhospitable to most life forms and have no surface to land on.

Desert.title: Desert planet
Desert.description: A very dry and sandy planet with an atmosphere that can range from extremely inhospitable to fairly mild. Most desert type planets have little to no vegetation but can still be capable of supporting life.

Water.title: Water planet
Water.description: This planet is composed of mostly water. These planets can sometimes have small landmasses or islands which may be safe to land on. However, due to their varying chemical compositions, most water on these worlds is not suitable for drinking.

Magma.title: Magma planet
Magma.description: A molten rock and lava covered planet. Extremely hot and inhospitable to most life forms. Special equipment is typically required to travel here.

YellowDwarf.title: Yellow dwarf
YellowDwarf.description: A small, warm and stable star that emits a lot of light for it's humble size.

WhiteDwarf.title: White dwarf
WhiteDwarf.description: A very small, but dense star that has exhausted most of its nuclear fuel. These start out very hot and gradually cool down.

RedDwarf.title: Red dwarf
RedDwarf.description: A small, cool and very stable star. These properties mean they are statistically the most numerous stars in The Slog.

RedGiant.title: Red giant
RedGiant.description: A giant, relatively cool star of low mass, that emits a lot of light. The most common birthing ground for planetary nebula.

BlueGiant.title: Blue giant
BlueGiant.description: A large, short lived, extremely hot star. Blue giants emit huge amounts of heat and radiation, making them very dangerous to travel near. However, extremely rare and valuable resources can sometimes be found orbiting these stars.
